description_long: This object is a tall, narrow bottle with a polygonal, almost hexagonal cross-section and a slightly tapered body. The bottle features straight, vertical sides that converge towards a small, pointed neck at the top, which is capped with a minimal, flat closure. The silhouette is symmetrical from both the front and side views, with the neck and cap being the only protruding elements. The base is flat, allowing the bottle to stand upright on surfaces. The exterior appears smooth and unadorned, with no visible labels, textures, or decorative features. The overall design suggests a utilitarian bottle, likely made from plastic or glass, suitable for holding liquids in urban environments.
description: A tall, narrow, polygonal bottle with a pointed neck and flat cap, featuring smooth, unadorned surfaces and a symmetrical silhouette.
description_view_0: Front view shows a straight, vertical silhouette with a slightly tapered body and no visible neck protrusion.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the pointed neck and cap, as well as the polygonal cross-section of the bottle.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, with a straight, vertical body and no visible neck protrusion.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again highlights the pointed neck and cap, matching the other side profile.
